zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Mortimer於1246年出生在英國賀福爾理查茲 卡素時，他的父親Hugh de Mortimer-2歲，母親Sibyl de Braose-1歲。他於1270年與Joyce de la Zouche在英國戴文結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1287年4月7日在英國武斯特斯爾維賀波勒德享年41歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾伍斯特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Mortimer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Mortemer in Seine-Maritime, France, so called from Old French mort(e) ‘dead’ + mer ‘sea’, probably referring to a stagnant pond or drained swamp.

Irish (of Norman origin): the Norman surname in sense 1 above was taken to Ireland from England in the medieval period, where it has also been adopted by bearers of the Gaelic surnames Mac Muircheartaigh and ÓMuircheartaigh, commonly Anglicized as McMurty and Mortagh. Compare McMurdo .

variants.name: Mortimore
meanings: English (Devon; of Norman origin): variant of   Mortimer  .

variants.name: McMurdo
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muircheartaigh or Mac Muircheartaich, ‘son of Muircheartach’, a personal name meaning ‘sea-ruler’. In Scotland the name was often lenited to give McCurdy . Compare Murdoch and Moriarty . The name was also used to Anglicize Mac Murchaidh (see McMorrow ).

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Mortimer was born in 1246, in Richards Castle, Herefordshire, England, his father, Hugh de Mortimer, was -2 and his mother, Sibyl de Braose, was -1. He married Joyce de la Zouche in 1270, in Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 7 April 1287, in Wychbold, Worcestershire, England, at the age of 41, and was buried in Worcester, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Mortimer(이)가 1246년 영국 허퍼드실르 리처스 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugh de Mortimer(은)는 -2세였고, 그의 어머니 Sibyl de Braose(은)는 -1세였다. 그는 1270년 영국 데폰에서 Joyce de la Zouche(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1287년 4월 7일 영국 워스스터실르 윈이ch볼드에서 41세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 워스스터에 묻혔다.
